# kombu-pyamqp-threadsafe

> Thread-safe extension of kombu's pyamqp transport. Upstream kombu (pyamqp)
> is designed "1 thread = 1 connection" with no connection sharing across
> threads; this package enables "1 thread = 1 channel", allowing one AMQP
> connection to be shared safely by many threads (e.g. one producer thread
> and one consumer thread on the same connection). It layers thread-safe
> Connection/Channel/ChannelPool classes over `amqp`/`kombu`, plus an
> optional dedicated background thread that owns the socket read.

Tech Stack: Python >=3.10 (tested 3.10-3.14), kombu >=5.6.1,<6 (pyamqp
transport, built on the `amqp` library), uv (build backend `uv_build`,
package manager), pytest + pytest-xdist for tests, ruff for lint/format,
mypy 1.x for typing, pre-commit + commitizen for commit/release hygiene,
RabbitMQ (via Docker) for integration tests, Toxiproxy for fault-injection
benchmarks.

## Architecture

### Module Hierarchy

```
src/
  kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe/
    __init__.py     # Core: ThreadSafeChannel, ThreadSafeChannelPool,
                     #   ThreadSafeConnection, KombuConnection,
                     #   Shared*Transport, monkeypatch/registration helpers
    drainer.py       # ConnectionDrainer, ConnectionClosedIntentionally,
                     #   DEDICATED_DRAINER_OPTION (optional dedicated IO thread)
    producer.py       # AutoChannelReleaseProducer (kombu.Producer subclass)
    simple.py          # SimpleQueue/SimpleBuffer with a race-safe .get()
  testing.py         # PropagatingThread (re-raises thread exceptions on join)
  toxiproxy_client.py # Minimal stdlib Toxiproxy HTTP client, used by benchmarks
  bench_metrics.py    # Latency/metrics collection helpers for benchmarks
  sentry_stacktrace_formatter.py  # Sentry event formatter (standalone utility)
tests/
  test_*.py                  # Unit/integration tests (require RabbitMQ; see conftest)
  test_connection_drainer.py # ConnectionDrainer unit tests against a fake socket
  test_drainer_integration.py, test_drainer_reconnect.py  # Drainer + real broker
  benchmarks/                # pytest-benchmark-style perf/fault-injection suite
    fixtures/                # toxiproxy + metrics pytest fixtures
project/
  frameworks/, research/     # Internal competency notes / research docs, not part
                              # of the library's public API or runtime behavior
```

Dependency direction: `kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe/__init__.py` is the core —
`producer.py` and `simple.py` are imported lazily from `KombuConnection`
methods (`Producer()`, `SimpleQueue()`, `SimpleBuffer()`) to avoid import
cycles. `drainer.py` is imported eagerly by `__init__.py` and depends only
on the standard library plus `amqp` exception types — it has no import-time
dependency back on `__init__.py` (only a `TYPE_CHECKING`-only reference to
`ThreadSafeConnection`), so the drainer can be reasoned about in isolation.
`testing.py`, `bench_metrics.py`, `toxiproxy_client.py` are test/benchmark
support, not shipped as part of the library's public surface.

### Data Flow — legacy path (dedicated_drainer=False, default)

Any application thread calling `drain_events()` becomes the (temporary)
socket reader: `DrainGuard` ensures only one thread reads the socket at a
time (others wait on a condition variable for that read to finish), then
`ThreadSafeConnection._execute_and_finish_drain` performs the actual
`amqp.Connection.drain_events()` under `_transport_lock` and dispatches
channel-0 (connection-level) events immediately. Frames for other channels
land in `channel_frame_buff[channel_id]` via `on_inbound_method` and are
dispatched only to the thread that owns that channel
(`channel_thread_bindings`), never cross-thread.

### Data Flow — dedicated drainer thread (transport_options={"dedicated_drainer": True})

A single background thread (`ConnectionDrainer`) becomes the connection's
only socket reader for its whole life. Application threads never read the
socket in this mode; they block on an activity generation counter until the
drainer has buffered something for them to dispatch.

```
                     ConnectionDrainer thread                      Application thread(s)
                     (owns_current_thread==True)                   (Producer / Consumer / drain_events())

  stop() from any    ---write 1 byte--->  self-pipe (_wakeup_w)
  other thread                                  |
                                                 v
                                        selectors.select([conn_sock,
                                                           wakeup_r], timeout)
                                                 |
                       +-------------------------+-------------------------+
                       | wakeup_r fired           | conn_sock readable      |
                       | -> stop, exit loop        | -> _drain_until_dry()   |
                       v                          v
                _terminate()             read one AMQP method via
                (wakes ALL waiters,       conn._drain_into_buffers()
                 sets _terminated)        (DrainGuard + _transport_lock,
                                            single reader invariant)
                                                 |
                                                 v
                                     buffer into conn.channel_frame_buff
                                     [channel_id]  (NO dispatch here)
                                                 |
                                                 v
                                        _notify_activity()
                                     (bump _generation, condvar.notify_all)
                                                                        |
                                                                        v
                                                          drain_events() thread was
                                                          blocked in wait_activity(),
                                                          wakes on generation change
                                                                        |
                                                                        v
                                                          dispatch its own buffered
                                                          frames itself (channel owner
                                                          thread dispatches — Rule 2)

  heartbeat ticker (same loop, interleaved with select):
    next_hb_at due -> _tick_heartbeat() -> conn.heartbeat_tick() under
    _transport_lock -> on ConnectionForced/OSError -> _fail(exc)

  fatal error path (socket error, heartbeat failure, unexpected crash):
    _fail(exc) stores exc, conn.mark_for_teardown(), notifies waiters (no
    generation bump) -> every wait_activity() re-raises the same exc

  intentional close path (application close()/collect()):
    KombuConnection.collect() -> ThreadSafeConnection.collect() ->
    drainer.stop(intentional=True) -> _intentional_stop=True BEFORE waking
    -> self-pipe write -> loop exits -> _terminate() -> any thread parked in
    wait_activity() raises ConnectionClosedIntentionally (NOT retried by
    kombu's ensure()/Consumer, unlike a recoverable error)
```

Key invariant carried over from the legacy path: the drainer only buffers
frames — it never dispatches, and never calls `collect()`/reconnects
itself. Dispatch still happens exactly where it always did (the thread that
owns the channel), and only an application thread ever drives teardown or
reconnect. This keeps **Rule 2** (see Key Patterns) intact and avoids the
drainer's own thread trying to join itself during teardown.

## Key Patterns

### One reader at a time via a guard, dispatch by channel owner (Rule 1 & 2)

Every read path — legacy `DrainGuard` or the dedicated drainer's
`_drain_into_buffers` — funnels through a single-reader gate, and every
dispatch happens only in the thread that owns the channel
(`channel_thread_bindings`). This is why `queue_declare(passive=True)`
failures surface as an exception in the *calling* thread, not a random
other thread reading the socket at the time.

```python
# src/kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe/__init__.py — ThreadSafeConnection.on_inbound_method
def on_inbound_method(self, channel_id, method_sig, payload, content):
    if self.channels is None:
        raise amqp.exceptions.RecoverableConnectionError("Connection already closed")
    # collect all frames to late dispatch (after drain) — never dispatch here
    self.channel_frame_buff[channel_id].append((method_sig, payload, content))
```

### channel_operation: snapshot-then-use around a possibly-tearing-down connection

Any `ThreadSafeChannel` method that touches `self.connection` (publish,
`basic_ack`, `queue_declare`, ...) wraps the call in `channel_operation`
(`ChannelOperation`/`ChannelCoordinator`). It takes one connection snapshot,
registers as an "active operation" so a concurrent `collect()` will wait for
it (up to `CHANNEL_TEARDOWN_WAIT_S`), and raises a `RecoverableConnectionError`
fast if teardown already started — never an `AttributeError` on a nulled
connection. Used across every AMQP method wrapper in `ThreadSafeChannel`
(`basic_publish`, `basic_get`, `queue_declare`, `exchange_declare`, ...).

```python
# src/kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe/__init__.py — pattern used by every channel method
def queue_declare(self, *args, **kwargs):
    with channel_operation(self):
        return super().queue_declare(*args, **kwargs)
```

### `_transport_lock` serializes all socket access, in both modes

`ThreadSafeConnection.frame_writer`, `blocking_read`, `connect`, `close`,
and the dedicated drainer's `_drain_into_buffers`/`_tick_heartbeat` all take
`_transport_lock` around the actual socket call. This is what makes
"1 connection, N threads" safe: writes are serialized per-call regardless
of which thread or mode is publishing, and a `KombuConnection`-level
`_transport_lock` additionally guards lazy transport/channel/pool creation
(`transport`, `default_channel`, `default_channel_pool` properties).

### Lazy import of Producer/Simple* to avoid cycles

`KombuConnection.Producer()`, `SimpleQueue()`, `SimpleBuffer()` import
`producer.py`/`simple.py` inside the method body rather than at module
top, keeping `__init__.py` free of a circular import while still using the
thread-safe connection everywhere.

## Module Responsibilities

- `ThreadSafeChannelPool` (`__init__.py`) → pool of `ThreadSafeChannel`, reassigns channel ownership to the acquiring thread on `acquire()`
- `ThreadSafeChannel` (`__init__.py`) → per-thread-bound `kombu.transport.pyamqp.Channel`; every socket-touching method wrapped in `channel_operation`
- `DrainGuard` (`__init__.py`) → legacy-path single-reader gate: one thread drains, others wait on a condition variable and inherit its result/exception
- `ChannelCoordinator` / `ChannelOperation` (`__init__.py`) → tracks in-flight per-channel operations so `collect()` can wait for them to drain before nulling the connection
- `ThreadSafeConnection` (`__init__.py`) → `amqp` Connection subclass; buffers inbound frames per channel, owns `_transport_lock`, starts/stops the optional `ConnectionDrainer`, branches `drain_events()` between legacy and drainer mode
- `KombuConnection` (`__init__.py`) → public `kombu.Connection` subclass; thread-safe `transport`/`connected`/`default_channel`/`default_channel_pool`, forked `ensure()` that avoids reconnect ping-pong, `_connection_factory()` that stops the outgoing drainer before building a replacement
- `SharedPyamqpTransport` / `SharedPyamqpSSLTransport` (`__init__.py`) → transports whose `Connection` class is `ThreadSafeConnection`; registered under `shared+pyamqp`/`shared+amqp`/`shared+amqps` via `add_shared_amqp_transport()`, or applied globally via `monkeypatch_pyamqp_transport()`
- `ConnectionDrainer` (`drainer.py`) → optional dedicated background thread; single socket reader when enabled, self-pipe wakeup, frame buffering (no dispatch), own heartbeat ticker, terminal-state signalling to waiters
- `ConnectionClosedIntentionally` (`drainer.py`) → `amqp.exceptions.IrrecoverableConnectionError` subclass raised to `drain_events()` waiters on an intentional close, so kombu's `ensure()`/`Consumer` do not retry/reconnect
- `AutoChannelReleaseProducer` (`producer.py`) → `kombu.Producer` that releases its channel back to the pool on `revive()`/`release()`
- `SimpleQueue` / `SimpleBuffer` (`simple.py`) → `kombu.simple` variants whose `.get()` re-checks an internal buffer on `Empty` to avoid losing a message raced in from another thread
- `PropagatingThread` (`testing.py`) → `threading.Thread` that re-raises the target's exception when joined; used throughout the test suite for background threads
- `ToxiproxyClient` (`toxiproxy_client.py`) → stdlib-only HTTP client for the Toxiproxy fault-injection server, used by `tests/benchmarks/`
- `bench_metrics.py` → `LatencyMetrics`/`MetricsCollector`/`BenchmarkReporter` used by the benchmark suite to collect and report latency stats

## Configuration

Connections are constructed like a normal `kombu.Connection`, via
`kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe.KombuConnection(url, **kwargs)` or
`KombuConnection.from_kombu_connection(existing_connection)`.

`transport_options` (passed through to `kombu.Connection`) supports:

- `dedicated_drainer` (bool, default `False`) — enables the dedicated
  drainer thread described above. Backward compatible: omitted or falsy
  reproduces the pre-existing legacy behavior byte-for-byte. Read by
  `ThreadSafeConnection.__init__` via `DEDICATED_DRAINER_OPTION` and popped
  from kwargs before calling `super().__init__()`, so it never leaks into
  `amqp.Connection`'s own kwargs.

`KombuConnection(default_channel_pool_size=100, ...)` — size of the lazily
created `default_channel_pool` (a `ThreadSafeChannelPool`).

Environment variables (test-only, read by `tests/conftest.py`):
`PYTEST_RABBITMQ_USER`/`PASS`/`HOST`/`PORT` — RabbitMQ connection for the
test suite (defaults `guest`/`guest`/`127.0.0.1`/`5672`).
`KOMBU_PYAMQP_THREADSAFE_DEBUG` — when truthy (`1`/`true`/`t`/`y`/`yes`),
wraps internal locks in `LoggingLock` to log acquire/release timing and
flag slow ("STUCK") acquisitions; off by default.

To register the transport under a URL scheme, call
`kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe.add_shared_amqp_transport()` once at startup
(adds `shared+pyamqp`/`shared+amqp`/`shared+amqps` to
`kombu.transport.TRANSPORT_ALIASES`), or
`monkeypatch_pyamqp_transport()` to replace kombu's default pyamqp
transport globally instead of using a new scheme.

## Common Pitfalls

**Sharing a channel across threads.** Responses are channel-bound; a
different thread calling into a shared `default_channel` can receive a
response or exception meant for another thread. Solution: never use
`default_channel` from multiple threads — use `default_channel_pool` and
acquire one channel per thread.

**Passive `queue_declare` closing the channel from an unexpected thread.**
If a channel is (incorrectly) shared, a passive-declare failure closing the
channel can surface in whatever thread happened to be draining at the time.
Solution: channels are bound to their owning thread
(`channel_thread_bindings`) and frames are only dispatched to that thread —
follow Rule 1/Rule 2 and this class of bug cannot occur.

**Leaked self-pipe file descriptors with the dedicated drainer.** An
unclosed connection with `dedicated_drainer=True` keeps 2 file descriptors
(the self-pipe) open for the life of the process. Solution: always call
`close()`/`collect()` on connections you are done with.

**Dedicated drainer and `os.fork()`.** The drainer thread does not survive
a fork — a forked child inherits a `Thread` object whose `is_alive()` may
still report `True` even though no real thread exists in the child
(`ConnectionDrainer.is_running` guards against this via a pid check, but
the drainer itself is not usable post-fork). Solution: recreate the
connection in the child process after forking.

**Reconnect ping-pong / stale-error collect().** Upstream kombu's `ensure()`
unconditionally calls `collect()` on any recoverable connection error, which
can tear down a connection another thread already replaced. `KombuConnection.ensure()`
is a fork of kombu's implementation that snapshots `self._connection` before
calling `fun` and only collects if the connection is still the one that saw
the error.

**Confusing `ConnectionClosedIntentionally` with a real failure.** With the
dedicated drainer, an application-initiated `close()`/`collect()` raises
`ConnectionClosedIntentionally` (an `IrrecoverableConnectionError`) to any
thread blocked in `drain_events()`, and kombu's `ensure()`/`Consumer` will
NOT retry or reconnect on it — this is intentional. A genuine failure
(dropped socket, missed heartbeat) still raises a recoverable error and
reconnects as usual. Code that broadly catches `amqp` connection errors and
retries should be aware this one exception type is deliberately excluded
from `recoverable_connection_errors`.

**Busy-spinning on a fragmented frame body.** A blocking read with
`timeout=0` makes `amqp`'s `TCPTransport._read` spin at ~100% CPU while a
frame body trickles in over multiple TCP segments. The drainer avoids this
by using a small non-zero `_DEFAULT_READ_TIMEOUT_S` (0.1s) for
`_drain_into_buffers`, so a mid-frame stall blocks on `recv` (CPU idle) and
raises `socket.timeout` instead — `read_frame` re-prepends any partial
bytes, so no data is lost.

## Development

### Setup

```
uv sync
```
Installs the package plus the `dev` dependency group (pytest, ruff, mypy,
pre-commit, commitizen, etc.). Add `--group docs` for the mkdocs-based docs
toolchain. Requires Python >=3.10 (CI matrix: 3.10-3.14).

Integration/benchmark tests need a real RabbitMQ (and, for fault-injection
benchmarks, Toxiproxy in front of it):
```
docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up -d
```
exposes AMQP through Toxiproxy on `5672`, the Toxiproxy HTTP API on `8474`,
and the RabbitMQ management UI on `15672`. Point tests at it with
`PYTEST_RABBITMQ_HOST`/`PYTEST_RABBITMQ_PORT` (defaults assume
`127.0.0.1:5672` with `guest`/`guest`).

### Commands

All via `uv run poe <task>` (Poe the Poet; run `uv run poe` alone for the
full list):

- `uv run poe lint` — runs `pre-commit run --all-files` (ruff check/format,
  mypy, commitizen commit-msg check, uv lock check, misc pygrep/file hooks)
- `uv run poe test` — `coverage run` (pytest under coverage) + `coverage
  report` + `coverage xml`; equivalent to `pytest` alone but with coverage
  wrapping (see `[tool.coverage.run] command_line = "--module pytest"`)
- `uv run poe benchmark` — `pytest -m benchmark tests/benchmarks/`
- `uv run poe fmt` — `ruff check --fix-only` + `ruff format`
- `uv run poe docs` — generates API docs via `pdoc`
- `cz bump` — bumps version, updates `CHANGELOG.md`, tags the release
  (Conventional Commits + Commitizen + SemVer + Keep a Changelog)

Raw pytest also works directly: `uv run pytest` (default `addopts` in
`pyproject.toml` excludes benchmark tests via `-m 'not benchmark'`,
enables `--exitfirst --failed-first`, doctest-modules, and writes
`reports/pytest.xml`).

### Testing

- `pythonpath = ["src"]`, `testpaths = ["src", "tests"]` — tests can import
  `kombu_pyamqp_threadsafe`, `testing`, `bench_metrics`, `toxiproxy_client`
  directly.
- Most tests require a live RabbitMQ (`tests/conftest.py` fixtures:
  `rabbitmq_dsn`, `connection`, `queue_name`); CI spins up
  `rabbitmq:3.12` as a service container.
- The `connection` fixture supports indirect parametrization to opt into
  the dedicated-drainer transport instead of the legacy path:
  `@pytest.mark.parametrize("connection", [True], indirect=True)` — this is
  how the same test bodies (e.g. `test_drainer_reconnect.py`) exercise both
  code paths without duplication.
- `tests/test_connection_drainer.py` unit-tests `ConnectionDrainer` against
  a fake socket-pair transport (`FakeTransport`) — no broker needed.
  `tests/test_drainer_integration.py` / `test_drainer_reconnect.py` exercise
  it against a real broker.
- Markers: `benchmark` (deselected by default, run explicitly with `-m
  benchmark`), `slow`, `stress`.
- `tests/benchmarks/` holds latency/throughput/fragmentation/reconnect
  benchmarks and Toxiproxy-based fault-injection tests; not part of the
  default test run.
- Coverage config (`pyproject.toml`) measures `src/` only, excluding
  `bench_metrics.py`, `toxiproxy_client.py`, `testing.py`.

The repo also tracks internal planning/competency notes under
`project/frameworks/` and `project/research/` (an internal knowledge base
for contributors); these are not part of the library's public API.

---
*Generated by documentation-keeper on 2026-07-15, based on commit `e5862b7` (docs(project): add DPF competency frameworks and kombu Hub research)*
